name: Vacuum birefringence from astrophysical polarization
requirement: Any candidate framework must respect the absence of significant vacuum birefringence in photon propagation, as constrained by polarization observations of distant astrophysical sources. Linear polarization of gamma-ray bursts and active galactic nuclei sets bounds on CPT-odd Lorentz-violating photon couplings (k_AF) at |k_AF| < 10^-43 GeV.

regime.description: Cosmological photon propagation distances; from radio polarimetry of AGN to optical polarization of GRBs.

current_bound.value: |k_AF| < 1e-43 GeV; CPT-even coefficients |(k_F)| < 10^-37 to 10^-32 depending on direction
current_bound.experiment: Polarization measurements of high-redshift sources (AGN, GRBs) collated in Kostelecky-Russell SME data tables
current_bound.reference: Kostelecky and Mewes, 'Astrophysical Tests of Lorentz and CPT Violation with Photons', ApJ 689, L1 (2008), arXiv:0809.2846; Kostelecky and Russell, 'Data Tables for Lorentz and CPT Violation', RMP 83, 11 (2011), updated annually at arXiv:0801.0287

what_it_excludes: Astrophysical polarization complements GRB-dispersion bounds (lorentz_invariance_photon entry) by constraining different SME coefficients. Vacuum birefringence would rotate polarization position angles in an energy-dependent way; the absence of such effects in distant sources rules out CPT-violating photon couplings at the 10^-43 GeV level.

parameterization.name: Standard Model Extension photon sector: CPT-odd 4-vector (k_AF)^kappa and CPT-even tensor (k_F)^kappa lambda mu nu coefficients.
parameterization.reference: Kostelecky and Mewes, PRD 66, 056005 (2002), arXiv:hep-ph/0205211; Kostelecky and Russell, RMP 83, 11 (2011).
parameterization.summary: Modifications of Maxwell equations parameterized by (k_AF), (k_F). Vacuum birefringence enters through the CPT-even tensor's parity-odd components.

procedural.predicted_observable: Energy-dependent polarization rotation phi(E) = (k_AF)^0 * L * E for CPT-odd; energy-dependent ellipticity for CPT-even effects.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: |k_AF^kappa| < 10^-43 GeV. Direction-dependent coefficients tabulated in arXiv:0801.0287.
framework_inputs: Modified photon dispersion / propagation derived from the framework's effective electromagnetic Lagrangian.
Decomposition into SME coefficients via standard formulas.

primary_references.citation: Kostelecky and Mewes, 'Astrophysical Tests of Lorentz and CPT Violation with Photons', ApJ 689, L1 (2008)
Kostelecky and Russell, 'Data Tables for Lorentz and CPT Violation', RMP 83, 11 (2011); updated annually
